steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The graph shows the correlation between the model's predicted ratings and P3's actual ratings: 0.17 for abstract paintings and 0.30 for cubist paintings. The question stipulates that P3 actually gave equal ratings to both styles. We need to find what the model predicted.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — P3 would derive more aesthetic pleasure from cubist paintings than from abstract paintings.: A higher correlation (0.30 vs 0.17) does not mean the model predicted higher ratings for cubist — correlation measures fit, not magnitude. ✗
  • Choice B — P3's ratings for abstract and cubist paintings would equal one another.: That describes P3's actual behavior (given in the premise), not what the model predicted. ✗
  • Choice C — P3 would derive less aesthetic pleasure from cubist paintings than from abstract paintings.: The lower correlation for abstract does not mean the model predicted lower cubist ratings. ✗
  • Choice D — P3's ratings for abstract and cubist paintings would differ from one another.: If P3 gave equal ratings but the model's correlation with P3 differed (0.17 vs 0.30), the model's predictions must have differed between the two styles — otherwise the correlations would be equal. ✓
That matches Choice D — P3's ratings for abstract and cubist paintings would differ from one another..
